skill.name: requesting-code-review
skill.description: Request and process code reviews with structured context after significant implementation work.
skill.summary: Prepare clear code review requests and process reviewer feedback.
skill.author: DMJGilbert

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown fences, review templates, and placeholder text as risky patterns. Manual review found no malicious intent, data exfiltration, prompt injection, or unsafe automation. The only real risk factor is low severity guidance to run read-only Git commands for review context.

low_findings.title: Read-only Git Commands in Documentation
low_findings.description: The skill tells users to run git log and git diff to gather review context. These commands are read-only and do not include user-controlled shell interpolation, but they are still external command guidance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are plainly visible in a bash example and are limited to local Git history and diff inspection. There is no evidence of network access, destructive flags, or command injection.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Hits Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain description text, prompt placeholders, and Markdown table content. No cryptographic function, hashing operation, or security-sensitive algorithm use appears at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are review request prose and placeholders, not executable code. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, or other weak cryptographic algorithm usage.

low_findings.title: Network Reconnaissance Static Hits Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged lines are checklist items for unit and integration tests. They do not include host discovery, port scanning, network probes, or network command usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relevant text only asks whether tests pass. No evidence found for network reconnaissance behavior at these lines.

content.value_statement: Code reviews often miss context when changes are handed off too late or too vaguely. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code package review requests and process feedback consistently.
actual_capabilities: Identifies when a code review should be requested during implementation work.
Guides users to collect Git commit and diff context before review.
Provides a structured code review request template.
Defines severity-based actions for reviewer feedback.
Offers a response format for accepted, disputed, and deferred feedback.
limitations: Does not perform the code review by itself.
Requires accurate commit ranges, branch names, and changed file lists from the user.
Does not validate whether tests actually passed.
Does not replace repository-specific review policies.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Pull Request Review
use_cases.description: Create a clear request that explains the change, affected files, test coverage, and review concerns.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Subagent Review Work
use_cases.description: Send a focused review prompt after each significant implementation task in a multi-agent workflow.

use_cases.title: Process Reviewer Feedback
use_cases.description: Sort feedback by severity and document fixes, disagreements, and deferred items.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Review Request
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request after a small change
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the requesting-code-review skill to create a review request for my latest changes. Include the summary, changed files, tests run, and questions for the reviewer.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Feature Branch
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the requesting-code-review skill to prepare a review request for branch [branch-name] against [base-branch]. Highlight risky files and areas that need extra attention.

prompt_templates.title: Send Work to a Reviewer Agent
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the requesting-code-review skill to draft a code-reviewer task prompt for commits [base]..[head]. Include what changed, requirements, key files, and specific concerns.

prompt_templates.title: Respond to Review Feedback
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the requesting-code-review skill to organize this reviewer feedback by severity. Draft a response that lists fixes, justified disagreements, and deferred low-priority items.

output_examples.input: I finished a bug fix and need a review request.
output_examples.output: A concise review request with commits, branch, summary, changed files, tests completed, areas needing attention, and reviewer questions.

output_examples.input: The reviewer found one critical issue and two minor suggestions.
output_examples.output: A severity-based response that prioritizes the critical fix, records completed changes, and defers minor items with clear rationale.

output_examples.input: I disagree with a reviewer comment about the implementation approach.
output_examples.output: A structured disagreement response that gives technical evidence, explains trade-offs, and invites correction if the evidence is incomplete.

best_practices: Request reviews before merging or integrating significant work.
Include commit ranges, changed files, requirements, and test results in each request.
Address critical and high severity feedback before continuing to new tasks.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for review without explaining what changed.
Do not dismiss reviewer feedback without technical evidence.
Do not mark tests as passed unless they were actually run.

faq.question: Does this skill run a code review automatically?
faq.answer: No. It helps prepare review requests and process feedback, but another reviewer or review agent must inspect the code.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does the skill require Git?
faq.answer: It recommends Git commands for commit and diff context. Users can provide equivalent information manually.

faq.question: Can it help with pull request descriptions?
faq.answer: Yes. Its review request template covers summary, changed files, requirements, tests, concerns, and reviewer questions.

faq.question: How should critical feedback be handled?
faq.answer: Critical feedback should be fixed immediately and should block other work until resolved.

faq.question: Is this skill repository-specific?
faq.answer: No. It provides a general review workflow that should be adapted to each repository's process.
